title: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread
wikitext:
[]: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by Pluto » Mon Mar 15, 2021 8:45 am Hi Everyone! I’m back with another plot summary thread that answers a lot of questions that people have been asking in regards to the climax of the film.I will continue to update this topic so stay tuned! Certainly, if I’ve missed anything, got anything wrong, or translated awkwardly, please feel free to let me know! I hope that the topics mentioned here will lead to some fruitful discussion in other topics on the board and among distinguished members of the community. Special thanks goes out to evageeks founder Reichu who has also helped me a lot with these topics. She has an uncanny knack for detail. Especially with the visual language of Evangelion. Background Again, I have relied on and translated Tsuchi-san’s “10 minute understanding of the Climax of Shin-Eva” video . So please check out his channel for more information. He’s seen the movie four times and everything he talks about is in the movie. I have also seen the movie and can confirm everything he’s saying. Although this post is long, it will answer many of the questions about what happens during the climax of the film. My comments/notes will be written in brackets []. Introduction After living in the third village, Shinji prepares himself for the worst. The Wunder sets off, and fights the black Wunder. Asuka arrives at NERV’s Antarctic headquarters and is snatched away by Unit 13. Gendo steals unit 01 and Mari and Shinji head towards the minus space together. Let’s explain step by step about how to go about entering this minus space.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Mar 15, 2021 1:39 pm Thanks, Pluto! This is some great info right here, full of rather intriguing material...

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Mar 15, 2021 2:49 pm Is the base in Antarctica really called the Calgary base?

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Ah Q » Mon Mar 15, 2021 3:38 pm hui43210 wrote: Is the base in Antarctica really called the Calgary base?

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Pluto » Mon Mar 15, 2021 5:13 pm Ah Q wrote: hui43210#915758 wrote: Is the base in Antarctica really called the Calgary base?

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Mar 15, 2021 6:38 pm Quite a fascinating read! Can't wait to see this for myself

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Tue Mar 16, 2021 3:16 am Pluto wrote: Ah Q#915770 wrote: I think it's supposed to be Calvary, not Calgary.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Mar 16, 2021 8:39 am They do this time around, yeah.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by EscapismIsBad » Wed Mar 17, 2021 4:26 pm Why Shinji eyes turn into purple? What's the context behind it? He does it out of will?

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Mar 17, 2021 4:32 pm DantesInferno wrote: Calgary is almost on the other side of the world isn’t it?

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Wed Mar 17, 2021 4:44 pm If Calvary Base was the site of Second Impact and essentially defunct afterwards, then Golgotha Base was just reusing the name X amount of years later. Personally, I've been wondering if there's a connection between Golgotha Base (home of the Dummy Plug) and the Golgotha Object.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax: Secrets Revealed             Quote Post by Pluto » Wed Mar 17, 2021 5:49 pm Gendo's Glasses wrote: If Calvary Base was the site of Second Impact and essentially defunct afterwards, then Golgotha Base was just reusing the name X amount of years later. Personally, I've been wondering if there's a connection between Golgotha Base (home of the Dummy Plug) and the Golgotha Object.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax             Quote Post by Raikyu » Thu Mar 18, 2021 11:55 am Well, that was an amazing and interesting read! I think that once we're able to watch the movie, that text will have an even higher value. With these explanations new questions arise, especially questions about the roles of the adams, lilith and the evangelions in the lore and how these concepts relate to each other. For example, Lilith in the end was kind of irrelevant, contrary to the original show and EoE. Or which angelic being is the basis of each evangelion? I hope that watching this movie or an expanded blu-ray edition will help to solve these mysteries. Edit: Also the role of Kaworu is a complete incognita too.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion's Climax             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Thu Mar 18, 2021 1:38 pm As soon as I read on the leaks about the Golgotha Object I assumed it was the GUFO.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 20, 2021 8:20 pm I like how this recalls this old gnostic belief about how the resurrection of jesus was actually "just below the moon" (in ancient times ppl believed that the moon, planets etc. where embedded in spheres and that up there there are copies of things in our world), like in some mythical place in the sky. It has a very "unknown phenomenon-y" name- It's also interesting that it's implied to have been left there by the FAR somehow. We can assume the same about the hex pillars etc and loads of other stuff that was "excavated" but it is a pity if there's little elaboration.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Sun Mar 21, 2021 5:59 am Kendrix wrote: It's also interesting that it's implied to have been left there by the FAR somehow. We can assume the same about the hex pillars etc and loads of other stuff that was "excavated" but it is a pity if there's little elaboration.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Sun Mar 21, 2021 7:22 am Just one thing from my viewing of the film I didn't see mentioned: At some point (I can't remember if it is before the start of or during Fourth Impact), Misato releases a series of Hex pillar satellites into space from the WUNDER. While Shinji is rejecting Instrumentality/Eva, the Hex satellites appear to glow, purifying the whole of Earth, hence why the planet is returned to normal (land and sea) when Shinji and Mari come out of the water. So while Shinji/Yui/Gendo ended Eva, Misato effectively purified the Earth. It's also a callback to and a reversal of the sequence in EoE where the MPEs hover in space as Instrumentality takes place.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 21, 2021 7:37 am interesting concept, makes it more of a collaborative effort if Misato's gonna blow herself up it should at least matter - now if only it was properly explained. Alas we never rly got exposition on the hex pillars. To some extent they're self-explanatory but they're so common at this point.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sun Mar 21, 2021 2:34 pm roblucci01 wrote: Just one thing from my viewing of the film I didn't see mentioned: At some point (I can't remember if it is before the start of or during Fourth Impact), Misato releases a series of Hex pillar satellites into space from the WUNDER. While Shinji is rejecting Instrumentality/Eva, the Hex satellites appear to glow, purifying the whole of Earth, hence why the planet is returned to normal (land and sea) when Shinji and Mari come out of the water. So while Shinji/Yui/Gendo ended Eva, Misato effectively purified the Earth. It's also a callback to and a reversal of the sequence in EoE where the MPEs hover in space as Instrumentality takes place.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion Climax Summary Thread             Quote Post by Pluto » Sun Mar 21, 2021 10:43 pm Kendrix wrote: I like how this recalls this old gnostic belief about how the resurrection of jesus was actually "just below the moon" (in ancient times ppl believed that the moon, planets etc. where embedded in spheres and that up there there are copies of things in our world), like in some mythical place in the sky. It has a very "unknown phenomenon-y" name- It's also interesting that it's implied to have been left there by the FAR somehow. We can assume the same about the hex pillars etc and loads of other stuff that was "excavated" but it is a pity if there's little elaboration.
